name: Deep-Fried Devils (You Zha Gui or You Tiao)
recipeIngredient: Unbleached flour (about 3 and a half cups)
Peanut or corn oil for deep frying
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve the alum, ammonium carbonate powder, baking soda, and baking powder in a large mixing bowl with water until all powders are dissolved.
2. Gradually add the flour to the mixture and mix using your hands in a pressing and pushing motion. Continue adding flour until the dough is firm enough to handle.
3. Transfer the dough to a floured surface and knead until smooth, with no lumps. Kneading should take no longer than 2 or 3 minutes.
4. Divide the dough into two pieces and shape them into oblong loaves. Coat the loaves with oil and wrap tightly in plastic.
5. Let the loaves sit at room temperature for at least 4 hours, up to 8 hours.
6. On a floured cutting board, stretch each loaf so that it measures 14 inches long and 3 inches wide. Place them on the board at least 4 inches apart and sprinkle with flour.
7. Roll one of the loaves out using a rolling pin until it is about 1/4 inch thick, 4 inches wide, and 16 inches long. Repeat with the other loaf. Cover with a damp towel and let rest for 10 minutes.
8. Heat oil in a deep fryer or heavy pot to 350°F (175°C). Keep paper towels nearby.
9. Cut 2 crosswise strips about 2/3 inch wide from one of the loaves. Brush the tops of these strips with water, then lay the remaining two strips on top. Press a chopstick lengthwise on each pair of strips to hold them together. Stretch each double strip until it is about 8 to 10 inches long.
10. Lower the pieces into the hot oil gently and fry until all sides are lightly browned, about 2 minutes. Remove with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels.
11. Repeat steps 9-10 with the remaining dough. Wrapped in plastic, the fried devils can be stored for up to 1 week refrigerated or 1 month frozen. Reheat in a preheated oven at 450°F (230°C) for about 2 minutes until crisp but not dry.
